yes: Good to see another serviceman, mate.
Glad to see you're still in uniform, soldier.
Keeping it together in this bloody mess, are we?
no: Oi! Civilian, this is a restricted area.
Careful now, this area isn't safe for civilians.
You shouldn't be wandering around here, mate.

responses.text: What happened to the rest of the British forces?
Do you have any supplies to trade?

dynamic_line: Private Jenkins, 2nd Battalion, Royal Regiment of Fusiliers.
Lance Corporal Williams, Royal Anglian Regiment.
Corporal Smith, Duke of Lancaster's Regiment.
I'm with what's left of the British Army in this godforsaken place.

responses.text: How did you end up here?

dynamic_line: Standing guard, what does it look like?
Keeping watch for hostiles. This whole bloody country's gone to hell.
Following orders. Supposed to secure this area and wait for reinforcements.
Trying to stay alive, same as everyone else these days.

responses.text: Have you seen any other military units?

dynamic_line: Most of our lads were deployed when everything went pear-shaped. Lost contact with command days ago.
It's a right mess. Some units were trying to evacuate civilians, others were containing those... things. Haven't heard from HQ in weeks.
We were on a joint exercise with the Americans when it happened. Our CO ordered us to secure this position and wait for orders. Still waiting...
There's supposed to be a rally point at the nearest RAF base, but the roads are crawling with those monsters.

responses.text: Is there any chain of command left?

dynamic_line: Last I heard, there was a Lieutenant Colonel trying to organize survivors near Manchester. Don't know if he's still alive.
Our Lieutenant is still giving orders, but I think he's making it up as he goes. Can't blame him, really.
Chain of command? That's a laugh. It's every unit for themselves now. Some of the lads have gone AWOL, can't say I blame them.
There's talk of some brass holed up in a bunker somewhere, but I'll believe it when I see it.
Was stationed at a base not far from here when everything went to hell. Most of my unit didn't make it.
We were deployed as part of a NATO exercise. Then those things started appearing everywhere. It was chaos.
I was on leave when it happened. Tried to get back to my unit, but... well, here I am instead.
Our transport crashed after the pilot turned into one of those things mid-flight. Only a few of us survived.
Yeah, well, that's life now, innit? Just got to crack on.
Could be worse. At least I'm still breathing.
Thanks. Lost a lot of good mates that day.
It is what it is. No use dwelling on it.

dynamic_line.yes: I've already told you, I can't spare anything right now.
no: I'm not authorized to trade military supplies, mate.
Sorry, need everything I've got to stay alive.
Can't help you there. Supplies are strictly rationed.
I could lose my stripes for trading equipment. Talk to the Quartermaster if you find one.
